page: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz with 16 physical cores and a single thread per core configuration. The instance is provisioned with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. Local storage is provided via a bundled 500 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core and memory performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and bzip3 single-threaded decompression both rank in the top tier, placing in the top 10% of tested instances. Large-block memory bandwidth, both read and write, and single-threaded gzip compression fall into the strong category, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance metrics, including multi-threaded stress-ng and bzip3 decompression, align with the average tier, representing the middle 50% of benchmarks.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a compute-heavy profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring high integer and cryptographic performance without massive memory overhead. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16, and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. A key tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core loads compared to dedicated instances.

This hardware configuration is suited for compute-intensive tasks that benefit from strong single-thread execution and fast local storage. Appropriate workloads include web serving, build systems, single-threaded data processing, and moderate database operations that fit within the 32.0 GB memory limit.
description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Genoa processor with x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 500 GB NVMe SSD. The CPU operates at 2.0 GHz with a shared allocation model. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU and bzip3 decompression performance, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth. However, multi-core CPU and database benchmarks remain in the average tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. This server is suited for compute-focused workloads, single-threaded data processing, and web serving applications that require fast local storage but do not demand high memory-to-CPU ratios.

tagline: A compute-optimized instance combining 16 AMD EPYC cores, fast NVMe storage, and strong single-threaded performance for efficient data processing.
bullet_points: 16 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
32.0 GB system memory and 500 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and bzip3 decompression performance
Support for AVX-512 vector and cryptographic instruction sets
Suited for web serving, single-threaded processing, and build systems
